methods.description: Process for collecting and preserving a specimen of the spottail spiny turbot for taxonomic and molecular analysis.

steps.description: A solitary specimen of the spottail spiny turbot, scientifically known as P. belcheri, was acquired from the estuaries of the Kineke River (latitude 2.938611 • N, longitude 9.911667 • E) located in Kribi, Cameroon.
steps.searchable_summary: collect specimen from Kineke River estuaries

steps.description: The identification process was meticulously carried out in accordance with available taxonomic keys.
Upon euthanizing the specimen with MS-222 (200 mg/L), muscle tissue was aseptically collected from the ventral thoracic region.
steps.searchable_summary: euthanize specimen and collect muscle tissue

steps.description: The voucher specimen was duly archived in 10% formaldehyde at the Fisheries and Animal Industries department (MINEPIA) in Yaoundé, Cameroon.
Simultaneously, a tissue sample was preserved at the Department of Marine Biology, Pukyong National University in Busan, South Korea.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve tissue sample at Pukyong National University

methods.description: Process for obtaining and analyzing global maps of Pleuronectiformes to understand the geographical distribution of P. belcheri and its congeners.

steps.description: Global maps of Pleuronectiformes, inclusive of the range distributions of P. belcheri and its congeners (P.erumei and P. bennettii), were obtained in .shpfile format from the IUCN database (https://www.iucnredlist.org/, accessed on 15 August 2023).
steps.searchable_summary: download Pleuronectiformes distribution maps from IUCN
